speaker: rep
text: Hi Sarah, thanks for taking my call today. I'm Jake from RepReady. How are you doing?

speaker: prospect
text: Hi Jake, doing well thanks. I've got about twenty minutes, so let's dive in.

speaker: rep
text: Perfect, I appreciate your time. So, um, I wanted to learn a bit about how your team currently handles sales onboarding.

speaker: prospect
text: Sure. Right now we have a two-week ramp program, but honestly reps still struggle on their first few calls.

speaker: rep
text: Got it. And, like, what does that struggle usually look like? Are they missing discovery questions or is it more about objection handling?

speaker: prospect
text: Mostly discovery. They jump straight into pitching without understanding the prospect's pain points.

speaker: rep
text: That makes sense. So basically your new reps tend to skip the needs assessment phase and go right into the product demo. That's actually a really common pattern we see across B2B sales teams. A lot of the companies we work with have the exact same problem where reps are so eager to show off the product that they forget to ask the right questions first. And you know what's interesting is that research shows the best discovery calls have at least a sixty-forty listen-to-talk ratio.

speaker: prospect
text: Yeah, that's exactly what happens. Do you have data on how coaching impacts ramp time?

speaker: rep
text: Absolutely. Um, so, like, our customers typically see a thirty percent reduction in ramp time.

speaker: prospect
text: That's interesting. What does your platform actually do differently from regular role-play sessions?

speaker: rep
text: Great question. We use AI to simulate realistic sales conversations and provide real-time coaching prompts.

speaker: prospect
text: How does the AI know what good looks like for our specific industry?

speaker: rep
text: So we have customizable coaching rules. You can configure detection for things like monologuing, filler words, missed questions, and feature dumping. The rules engine runs against a rolling window of the conversation.

speaker: prospect
text: That sounds useful. What's the pricing model?

speaker: rep
text: So the pricing is per seat per month. But before we get into that, I'd love to understand more about your team size. How many SDRs do you currently have?

speaker: prospect
text: We have about fifteen SDRs right now, planning to grow to twenty-five by Q3.

speaker: rep
text: Nice, that's solid growth. And who's currently responsible for coaching them? Is it the sales managers or do you have dedicated enablement staff?

speaker: prospect
text: It's mostly our two sales managers, but they're stretched thin between coaching and their own pipeline.

speaker: rep
text: Right, so your managers are basically, um, you know, like, juggling between their own deals and trying to develop the team. That's a tough spot.

speaker: prospect
text: Exactly. That's why we're looking at tools that can scale coaching without adding headcount.

speaker: rep
text: Makes total sense. Let me tell you about our analytics dashboard. It has real-time metrics, historical trend analysis, rep comparison charts, manager dashboards, exportable reports, integration with Salesforce, custom KPI tracking, and automated weekly summaries. Plus we just launched a mobile app.

speaker: prospect
text: That's a lot of features. Which ones would be most relevant for our use case?

speaker: rep
text: Right, sorry about that. For a team your size focused on reducing ramp time, I'd recommend starting with the real-time coaching and the rep scorecard features.

speaker: prospect
text: That makes more sense. Can we set up a pilot with a few reps to test it out?

speaker: rep
text: Absolutely, we can do a two-week pilot. I'll send over the details after this call. Thanks for your time today, Sarah.
